mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs within the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal fails to predict direction during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint increases in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output that supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the mechanism shows negative predictive power when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls signal robust economic activity that boosts investor confidence and produces positive S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases correlate with negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of observations, or the signal Rank IC drops below zero over a 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 195.0, persistent inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts anticipatory monetary tightening, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 195.0 fail to predict negative 60-day returns, or forward returns remain positive in > 55% of high-inflation observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly increases in retail sales confirm synchronized demand expansion that reliably generates positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising GDPC1 and RSAFS yields negative 20-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index during late-cycle expansions signal manufacturing contraction that precedes immediate equity market corrections within the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle expansions, or the signal produces positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances.
